message:
refactor(storage): collapse ready-query bucket split, add import row helpers

This is a two-part rework of src/storage/sqlite.rs that lands together
because both halves touch the same `SqliteStorage` impl block.

Part 1 — ready candidate query simplification
----------------------------------------------
Removes the SQL-side priority bucket split that 46ef9076 introduced
(`ReadyPriorityBucket::High` / `::Low` plus the dedicated
`idx_issues_ready_created` partial index) and the
`ReadyFlagPredicate::ExactFalse` / `::LegacyNullAsFalse` enum that
toggled NOT-NULL fast paths on the pinned/ephemeral/is_template
columns.

Why: the bucket split executed two SQL queries with different ORDER BY
shapes plus result merging in Rust, and the NOT-NULL fast path required
sniffing PRAGMA table_info(issues) on every call. Both layers added
substantial code surface and correctness risk for a microbench that
only mattered when `ready --limit N` happened to fall right at the
P0/P1 → P2+ boundary. The hybrid sort is still preserved — it now
runs as a Rust-side stable sort over the raw candidate set (see
`sort_ready_hybrid` + `ready_hybrid_bucket`) when no SQL `LIMIT` is
provided, and as the existing CASE-based ORDER BY when a LIMIT is
present (so a small `--limit` still pushes the cut into SQL).

Concrete code changes:
- Drop `ReadyFlagPredicate`, `ReadyPriorityBucket`,
  `ready_filter_columns_are_not_null`,
  `query_ready_issue_candidates_dispatch`, and
  `query_ready_hybrid_issue_candidates_with_projection`.
- `build_ready_issue_candidates_query` now takes `apply_ordering: bool`
  in place of the predicate/bucket parameters; pinned/ephemeral/
  is_template always use the `(col = 0 OR col IS NULL)` form so legacy
  rows that still store NULL behave correctly.
- `query_ready_issue_candidates_with_projection` decides whether to
  sort in Rust based on `sort == Hybrid && filters.limit.is_none()`.
- Add free-standing `sort_ready_hybrid(&mut [Issue])` and a
  `const fn ready_hybrid_bucket(Priority) -> i32` so the hybrid order
  is one place.

src/storage/schema.rs:
- Drop the now-unused `idx_issues_ready_created` partial index from
  both the bootstrap `SCHEMA_SQL` block and the `run_migrations`
  catch-up block, plus its `tests::indexes_present` assertion.

Part 2 — extract reusable import row helpers
--------------------------------------------
Pulls the closure-soup that `upsert_issue_for_import` used to inline
out into named helpers so JSONL import can pick the right path
without rebuilding the column-value vector every call:

- New `ImportIssueTimestampStrings` struct (`from_issue(&Issue)`)
  caches the rfc3339 conversions for created_at / updated_at / closed_at /
  due_at / defer_until / deleted_at / compacted_at — the closure was
  doing this work on every iteration.
- New `Self::import_issue_field_values(issue, &timestamps)` returns
  the column-order `Vec<SqliteValue>` shared by INSERT and UPDATE.
- New `insert_issue_row_for_import` and `update_issue_row_for_import`
  wrap the two SQL forms.
- New `pub(crate) insert_new_issue_for_import(&self, &Issue) -> Result<bool>`
  is the fast path: it skips the existence probe entirely so JSONL
  import on a fresh database does one INSERT instead of probe + branch.
  The caller (sync/mod.rs) handles primary-key collisions explicitly
  by falling back to `upsert_issue_for_import` so the existing
  upsert semantics are preserved when collisions actually happen.
- `upsert_issue_for_import` now reuses these helpers instead of
  carrying its own copy of the column closure; the
  `#[allow(clippy::too_many_lines)]` is no longer needed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -90,15 +90,6 @@ pub const SCHEMA_SQL: &str = r"
         AND pinned = 0
         AND is_template = 0;
 
-    -- Hybrid ready order split path: P0/P1 by created_at, then all others
-    -- by created_at.  Keeps both bucket scans ordered without a temp sort.
-    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_ready_created
-        ON issues(status, created_at)
-        WHERE status = 'open'
-        AND ephemeral = 0
-        AND pinned = 0
-        AND is_template = 0;
-
     -- Common active list path: non-terminal issues sorted by priority/created_at.
     -- Uses ASC on created_at (not DESC) to avoid frankensqlite B-tree ordering
     -- divergence with C sqlite3 integrity_check.  SQLite reverse-scans the ASC
@@ -1279,13 +1270,6 @@ fn run_migrations(conn: &Connection, issues_rebuilt: bool) -> Result<()> {
             AND pinned = 0
             AND is_template = 0;
 
-        CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_ready_created
-            ON issues(status, created_at)
-            WHERE status = 'open'
-            AND ephemeral = 0
-            AND pinned = 0
-            AND is_template = 0;
-
         -- Common active list path: non-terminal issues sorted by priority/created_at
         CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_list_active_order
             ON issues(priority, created_at)
@@ -1922,10 +1906,6 @@ mod tests {
             indexes.contains("idx_issues_ready"),
             "missing idx_issues_ready composite index"
         );
-        assert!(
-            indexes.contains("idx_issues_ready_created"),
-            "missing idx_issues_ready_created hybrid index"
-        );
         assert!(
             indexes.contains("idx_issues_list_active_order"),
             "missing idx_issues_list_active_order composite index"
